canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, MultiRC is consistently framed as a benchmark dataset for "Multi-Sentence Reading Comprehension." The most prevalent description characterizes it as a multiple-choice dataset where questions require reasoning over or deriving answers from multiple sentences within a text. One paper adds that questions may have multiple correct answers, and another specifies that it is evaluated with exact-match accuracy. The provided data indicates that MultiRC is used to measure two constructs: "Reading comprehension" and "Faithfulness." In practice, it is used as a task to compare the performance and efficiency of different models and methods. For example, one study notes that "Successful fine-tuning on MultiRC implies Addax can handle smaller tasks as well" ("Addax: Utilizing Zeroth-Order Gradients to Improve Memory Efficiency and Performance of SGD for Fine-Tuning Language Models").

Enhancing Zeroth-order Fine-tuning for Language Models with Low-rank Structures
Mini-batch Coresets for Memory-efficient Language Model Training on Data Mixtures
RegMix: Data Mixture as Regression for Language Model Pre-training
Second-Order Fine-Tuning without Pain for LLMs: A Hessian Informed Zeroth-Order Optimizer
Predictive Data Selection: The Data That Predicts Is the Data That Teaches
Regress, Don’t Guess: A Regression-like Loss on Number Tokens for Language Models
BAdam: A Memory Efficient Full Parameter Optimization Method for Large Language Models

definitions.text: Multi-Sentence Reading Comprehension, a multiple choice dataset evaluated with exact-match accuracy.
